title: rosa woodsii var. gratissima
intro: Rosa woodsii var. gratissima, also known as the fragrant rose or Woods' rose, is a variety of rose species native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Rosa woodsii var. gratissima include reducing pain and inflammation, treating wounds and skin conditions, and alleviating digestive issues. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water or mixing the extracts with carrier oils to create topical applications.
